title: eryngium lacustre
intro: Eryngium lacustre, also known as Sea Holly or Sea Eryngo, is a perennial plant species native to Europe, known for its distinctive thistle-like appearance and blue or purple flowers. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which have been attributed to its alkaloid and flavonoid compounds. The health benefits of Eryngium lacustre include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve respiratory health, and act as an antioxidant. Herbal preparations of Eryngium lacustre can be found in the form of teas, tinctures, and infusions, often used to support immune function and relieve symptoms of colds and flu.
